title: crepidomanes clarenceanum
intro: Crepidomanes clarenceanum is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the Clarencean forked spleenwort. It is native to the Pacific Islands and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and improvement of digestive health. Herbal preparations of Crepidomanes clarenceanum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves, which can be consumed as a tea to reap its medicinal benefits.
